title: Port Congestion: A Shifting Focus?
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
The news that China is allowing eight vessels to berth and discharge their cargoes of Australian coking coal, after many months of waiting, have raised hopes among some observers that the ban on Australian imports may come to an end. It is not yet clear if it is the result of change in policy by Chinese authorities or if it is a humanitarian move to allow the crews on the vessels to be changed. The mix of panamaxes and capesizes will offload around 850,000 tonnes of coal in the two Chinese ports of Caofeidian and Jingtang.
